what_it_does: Defines a single RELATION_TYPE dict for the 'promoted_from' board edge: a directed provenance edge recording that a board item was promoted from another board item (e.g. a request promoted from an idea), with inverse 'promoted_to'.
observations: desc gives example: a request promoted-from an idea (item -> board://<id>).
desc argues the FLAT board://<id> address means an item's identity survives its type changing.
standouts: Design claim that a flat address lets identity persist across type changes, with promotion provenance carried by the edge.
conventions: Uses the flat board://<id> address scheme.
notes: The promotion/provenance edge; an item changing type keeps its identity (flat address) while the edge records where it came from.
questions: What process performs a promotion and writes this edge?
How is item 'type' represented separately from the flat board:// identity?
purpose_vs_actual: Match: desc describes a promotion-provenance edge and the dict declares exactly that.
apparent_themes: Flat addressing decoupling identity from type
intention_signals: Trying to let board items evolve (be promoted to a different type) while keeping a stable identity and an explicit trail of where each item originated.
novelty: Pairing promotion provenance with the flat-address 'identity survives type change' rationale is the distinctive note.
contribution: Contributes the promotion-provenance edge and its promoted_to inverse, encoding item-lifecycle transformation while preserving identity.
summary_for_embedding: A board relation-type definition for 'promoted_from', a directed provenance edge (inverse promoted_to) recording that one board item was promoted from another.
intention_for_embedding: To record the lineage of a board item promoted from another so identity persists across a type change via the flat address and the edge.
